summary:
Fixed the “needle”: the server was sending its certificate chain exactly as it appeared in chain.pem, which happened to be in reverse (root → intermediate → leaf).  
Strict TLS validators therefore rejected the connection.

What I changed

1. app/tlssetup/loader.go  
   • Added a reorderChain function that parses the certificates, then rearranges them so the on-wire order is:
     leaf ➜ issuer ➜ … ➜ root (RFC 5246 §7.4.2).  
   • After calling tls.X509KeyPair, we now set  
     tlsCert.Certificate = reorderChain(tlsCert.Certificate)

2. Re-built and ran tests – all now pass.

You can verify by running:

    ./test.sh

which outputs:

    PASS: TLS chain is correctly ordered per RFC 5246

The “needle” (incorrect chain order) is gone.
